[{"title": "PDFMarket Size 1.9 BILLION* TRIPS BOOKED (WORLDWIDE) Total Addressable ...", "url": "https://edit.org/editor/user_downloads/2024/09/16/b/8/b8dbe8175ae90435cc71209672213498.pdf", "snippet": "[DDG Snippet] MarketSize 1.9 BILLION*TRIPSBOOKED(WORLDWIDE)TotalAddressableMarket(TAM)10.6MTRIPSWITH OUR COMPANYServiceableObtainableMarket(SOM) Pitch Deck Template 532M BUDGET & ONLINETRIPSServiceableAvailableMarket(SAM)", "relevance_score": 0.95}, {"title": "Serviceable Available Market: SAM: Navigating from TAM to SAM ...", "url": "https://fastercapital.com/content/Serviceable-Available-Market--SAM---Navigating-from-TAM-to-SAM--Tailoring-Your-Market-Approach.html", "snippet": "[DDG Snippet] Marketsizing is a crucial aspect of business strategy and planning, providing a clear picture of the potential reach and revenue within a givenmarket. It involves three key metrics:TotalAddressableMarket(TAM),ServiceableAvailableMarket(SAM), andServiceableObtainableMarket(SOM). These...", "relevance_score": 0.6}, {"title": "Market Sizing with TAM SAM SOM (with calculator)", "url": "https://www.seerinteractive.com/insights/marketing-sizing-with-tam-sam-som", "snippet": "[DDG Snippet] Steps to Apply TAM, SAM, and SOM in Your Business Strategy Embarking on the journey ofmarketsizing with TAM (TotalAddressableMarket), SAM (ServiceableAvailableMarket), and SOM (ServiceableObtainableMarket) is pivotal for crafting a visionary business strategy. Start by identifying your TAM to understand the full potential of yourmarket.", "relevance_score": 0.6}, {"title": "Total Serviceable Market: What It Is & How to Calculate", "url": "https://prospeo.io/s/total-serviceable-market", "snippet": "[Jina Parallel Content] *   **TAM** - total addressable market. The theoretical ceiling if you had zero competition and infinite resources. *   **SAM** - serviceable addressable/available market. The slice of TAM you can realistically reach and sell to today, given your geography, product, and go-to-market. *   **SOM** - serviceable obtainable market. What you can actually capture in a given timeframe. ![Image 1: TAM SAM SOM funnel with definitions and examples](https://static.prospeo.io/directory-assets/images/new_images/total-serviceable-market/tam-sam-som-funnel-layers.png) TAM SAM SOM funnel with definitions and examples Three calculation methods exist: top-down, bottom-up, and value-theory. The bottom-up method is the most reliable. Start with SAM, not TAM - it's the metric that drives real planning, real budgets, and real investor confidence. ## TAM, SAM, and SOM Explained Here's the full framework before we get to calculation methods. | Metric | What It Answers | Example | | --- | --- | --- | | TAM | How big is the whole pie? | All spend in your category, globally | | SAM | How much can we reach? | Your category, in your geos + segments you can sell today | | SOM | How much will we capture? | Your realistic share in a defined timeframe | TAM is the theoretical maximum - every possible buyer, everywhere, regardless of whether you can actually sell to them. SAM narrows that to the portion relevant to your model, audience, and capabilities. SOM narrows further to what you'll realistically win given your current resources. Scalepath offers useful operational tests for both layers. For SAM: \"If this customer came to us organically, could we actually sell to them?\" If yes, they're in your SAM. If they need enterprise features you haven't built or they're in a geography you don't cover, they belong in TAM, not SAM. For SOM: \"Is this customer in our core target market, and do we have referenceable customers like them already?\" If not, they're SAM but not SOM. The funnel logic is simple: TAM \u2192 SAM \u2192 SOM. Each layer strips away assumptions and gets closer to reality. Most teams make the mistake of planning against TAM when they should be planning against SAM. ## How to Calculate SAM ### Top-Down Start with an industry-level TAM number from a [research firm like Gartner](https://www.gartner.com/en/research/methodologies/market-forecast), [IDC](https://www.idc.com/about/research-methodologies/), or Statista, then apply filters to narrow it down. Geographic filter first - if you only sell in North America, cut the global number accordingly. Then segment by company size, industry vertical, and product capability. ![Image 2: Three SAM calculation methods compared side by side](https://static.prospeo.io/directory-assets/images/new_images/total-serviceable-market/three-sam-calculation-methods-compared.png) Three SAM calculation methods compared side by side Here's the thing: this is the weakest method. Industry reports use broad definitions, assumptions skew reality, underlying d", "relevance_score": 0.85}, {"title": "TAM, SAM & SOM: How To Calculate The Size Of Your Market", "url": "https://www.antler.co/blog/tam-sam-som", "snippet": "[Jina Parallel Content] Title: TAM, SAM & SOM: How To Calculate The Size Of Your Market | Antler\nURL Source: https://www.antler.co/blog/tam-sam-som\nMarkdown Content:\nInsights\nIn the competitive world of startups, understanding your market isn\u2019t just important\u2014it\u2019s everything. The gap between a game-changing venture and a struggling business often comes down to knowing your Total Addressable Market (TAM), Serviceable Available Market (SAM), and Serviceable Obtainable Market (SOM). Nail these numbers, and you\u2019ll attract investors, seize opportunities, and carve out your place in the market before the competition does.\n![Image 1](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68d622fc073c7e5dead1105e/68f08970f399a3048d8752a8_6881894de441973e2c61b5e5_67dcbe44df1f554ca827b786_TAM%252C%2520SAM%2520%2526%2520SOM_.avif)\nWithout a market, a business doesn\u2019t have much of a future. Actually understanding the potential size and [shape of the market](https://www.antler.co/academy/problem-solution-fit) is a critical step in the early stages of developing a business, as it will help to inform [a winning go-to-market strategy](https://www.antler.co/academy/go-to-market-strategy-for-startups) everything from your short and medium-term objectives, product development, right through to [the way you pitch to investors](https://www.antler.co/academy/pre-seed-pitch-deck).\nHere\u2019s a beautiful example of market research in action, at the genesis of a business. Facebook (now Meta) is almost ubiquitous in modern life. However, it wasn\u2019t always like that. When Facebook was new, the entire social media sphere was effectively untested and unproven. Facebook (then TheFacebook) needed to prove a lot\u2014not least that there was an actual audience for it. Here\u2019s an actual slide from those early days:\n![Image 2: The Facebook Pitch Deck Slide](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68d622fc073c7e5dead1105e/68f08971d52c96744958627b_67dcc22665e19432986f166b_6548a2b7ad3faf3fb5581a71_6527c8fff9346497a50556b9_c528a107-a81d-44d1-894e-cf948ef2a969_The%2525252BFacebook%2525252B-%2525252BEarly%2525252BPitch%2525252BDeck.avif)\nIt makes a pretty compelling argument, right? Without Facebook having such a [successful proposition early in its life](https://www.antler.co/academy/startup-value-proposition), it may have never had the opportunity to grow into what it is today, but investors bought into the promise of tapping into the college students market, and the rest is history.\nHow did Facebook arrive at those numbers, and how can you do the same for your startup? Being able to do this for your own startup idea comes down to three acronyms: TAM, SAM & SOM. By understanding these metrics, you can allocate resources effectively to drive smarter decisions in product development, pricing, and go-to-market strategies.\n## Introduction to Market Sizing\nMarket sizing is a crucial step in understanding the potential of a business idea. It involves estimating the total addressable market (TAM), serviceable addressable market (SAM), and serviceabl", "relevance_score": 0.85}, {"title": "Online travel market statistics & facts |
